feedback.text: Context- Hello there. I've been playing games all my life, but in the recent years more and more games I own have been shut down by video game companies, and my purchase has henceforce been in vain. The whole ordeal is pretty one sided. I did my part, purchased the game and given the company my money, but suddenly the company decides to shut down the servers the game is dependent on and I'm left with nothing. Even psysical games I have in my shelf doesn't work anymore. In according to this bills name, that doesn't feel fair to me. I don't demand that video game companies must support the game to the end of time, but that they make a last end of service patch to the game that either removes the dependency of the servers or make it possible for the video game community to set up new servers for the game. When a game today is shut down, it's unlikely neither of those options will be offered to the community. It's fully removed. Comparison; Like if you purchased a book, and when the time of the bookstores convenience, they come and take the book from us and burn it down so it will never be seen again. Takeaway - So in accordamce with this bills name. Digital Fairness Act. Please make it so that companies need to have a end of life plan for the game. When the game's servers are eventually shut down we still can play the game. And not unfairly have it taken away from us. Thank you.
